title: HP's Acquisition of Compaq: Beyond the Naysayers, the Promise and the Opportunity
author: Peter Kastner; Robert Dorin; Bill Claybrook; Aberdeen Group Research Team
abstract: This Aberdeen Group collection of seven InSight pieces examines the HP-Compaq acquisition from multiple perspectives: corporate strategy, PC business integration, Windows/Wintel strategy, Unix platform consolidation, Linux opportunities, storage strategy, and IT services mix. Peter Kastner, Aberdeen's Chief Research Officer, identifies significant upside potential for synergy while warning that execution excellence and message clarity must surpass pre-merger levels. The collection argues the combination could create an industry powerhouse if HP prioritizes customer outreach and sales force retraining.
importance_rationale: This multi-perspective Aberdeen collection represented one of the most comprehensive independent analyst assessments of the largest technology merger in history at the time. Peter Kastner's role as Aberdeen's Chief Research Officer gave the analysis significant market authority during a contested shareholder vote.
relevance_rationale: The frameworks for evaluating large technology M&A integrations, customer outreach strategies, and platform consolidation decisions remain highly applicable to current enterprise computing mergers. The specific technology landscape is dated but the strategic analysis methodology transfers directly.
prescience_rationale: Aberdeen correctly identified the PC business and clarity of message as critical success factors, and the merger did initially create cost savings exceeding targets. However, the 'industry powerhouse' vision was only partially realized; HP later split into HP Inc. and HPE in 2015, and Carly Fiorina was ousted in 2005, suggesting execution challenges Aberdeen warned about materialized.
